assertions.text: The truck left the roadway (departed the road surface) prior to any collision.
The truck struck a traffic signal pole (impact with a pole located alongside/adjacent to the road).
There was a single impact in the crash (one distinct collision event).
The single impact with the traffic signal pole occurred before the truck came to rest.
No additional impacts occurred after the truck came to rest.
The crash consisted of the truck hitting the traffic signal pole and then coming to rest (implying no subsequent collisions).

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows a pre-impact speed decline from [157.8 km/h](T2) at t=-5.0 s to [122.4 km/h](T2) at t=0.0 s and a detected large speed drop event from [157.8 km/h to 133.6 km/h over -3.5 to -0.5 s](T3), but there are no direct sensors that record 'road departure' (no GPS lateral position or lane data present), so departure from the roadway cannot be confirmed from the available channels.

The crash pulse shows cumulative longitudinal delta-V peaking at [7.3 km/h](T2) (final value [7.3 km/h](T2)) and lateral delta-V peaking at [4.3 km/h](T2), consistent with a side/angled impact, but the telemetry does not identify the struck object type or its roadside location (no object/pole sensor or absolute position), so asserting a traffic signal pole impact is not verifiable from sensors alone.

The EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded: 3](T1) and the detector found two separate crash-pulse delta-V detections for longitudinal ([7.7 km/h at t≈0.251 s](T3)) and lateral ([4.3 km/h at t≈0.21 s](T3)) plus multiple impact spikes, indicating more than a single recorded event rather than exactly one distinct collision.

The crash-pulse cumulative delta-V rises after t=0 with the longitudinal peak at [7.3 km/h at t≈0.24 s](T2) and the pre-impact speed at t=0.0 was [122.4 km/h](T2), showing the impact (delta-V pulse) occurs while the vehicle was still moving and before coming to rest; detectors show the brake was applied at [-0.5 s](T2) then the delta-V event occurs at t≈[0.21–0.251 s](T3), supporting that the impact occurred before the truck came to rest.

The decoded data show the crash-pulse delta-V final values are [7.3 km/h](T2) longitudinal and [4.1 km/h](T1) lateral (final), and the EDR summary lists no subsequent events after the crash pulse and reports [num_events_recorded: 3](T1) with the crash-pulse peaks occurring at t≈[0.21–0.30 s](T1), indicating no additional impacts were recorded after the vehicle reached its post-impact state.

Telemetry shows a single main crash pulse with cumulative delta-V magnitudes of about [7.3 km/h longitudinal](T2) and [4.3 km/h lateral peak](T2) and the vehicle speed just before impact was [122.4 km/h](T2), consistent with a single collision event that brought the vehicle to rest without recorded subsequent collisions.

result.rationale: Sensor data confirm a crash pulse (delta-V) with measured longitudinal ([7.3 km/h](T2)) and lateral ([4.3 km/h](T2)) components and a pre-impact speed trajectory ([157.8→122.4 km/h](T2)), and detectors report multiple recorded events ([num_events_recorded: 3](T1)). However, object identification (traffic signal pole) and explicit road-departure cannot be determined from available channels, so the narrative is partially verifiable and partially unverifiable.

citations.claim: 157.8 km/h to 133.6 km/h over -3.5 to -0.5 s

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
